message:
fix(pool): thread caller Cx through idle-mutex acquire (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Follow-up to tick 13 (commit b44020b4) which fixed the timeout binding.
This commit threads the caller's `Cx` through the idle-pool Mutex
acquire as well, closing the last divergent-path gap in
`Pool::acquire_with_cx` and `Pool::try_acquire_with_cx`.

Scope:

1. crates/frankenterm-core/src/pool.rs (+33 src, -25 src)

   a. `try_acquire_with_cx` no longer delegates to `try_acquire_inner`
      under `asupersync-runtime`. Instead it inlines the semaphore +
      idle-lock dance directly so it can call
      `self.idle.lock_with_cx(cx)` with the caller's Cx.

      Previously: `try_acquire_with_cx` → `try_acquire_inner` →
      `self.idle.lock()` (ambient Cx). A caller-cancelled Cx did
      NOT bind the mutex wait.

      Now: `try_acquire_with_cx` directly uses `lock_with_cx(cx)`
      so a caller-cancelled wait propagates through the full
      acquire path.

   b. `acquire_with_cx` (tick 13 already fixed the timeout path) now
      also uses `self.idle.lock_with_cx(cx).await` for the post-
      permit idle lookup. Same motivation: a caller-cancelled Cx
      should cut the idle mutex wait, not just the timeout future.

   c. `try_acquire_inner` is now gated to
      `#[cfg(not(feature = "asupersync-runtime"))]` since the
      asupersync path goes through `try_acquire_with_cx` which
      inlines the body. Avoids the `-D dead-code` clippy lint.

No behavior change for legacy `try_acquire` / `acquire` callers —
they still delegate through `try_acquire_inner` under non-asupersync
and through the Cx-first path (with `cx::for_request()` ambient Cx)
under asupersync-runtime.

Verification (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target):

  * cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib pool::: 179/179 pass
    (all existing tests; no new tests — contract coverage is
    already in place from tick 13 + tick 9's lock_with_cx LabRuntime
    tests)
  * cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --lib -D warnings: clean
  * rustfmt --check: clean

Leaves ft-xbnl0.2.3 still in_progress. Pool::acquire_with_cx /
try_acquire_with_cx are now fully Cx-first end-to-end — caller Cx
flows through the semaphore AND the idle mutex AND the timeout
disambiguation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
